title: IMD issues Red Alert for very heavy rainfall over Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu & Kashmir - News On AIR
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued red colour warning for heavy to very heavy rainfall with isolated extremely heavy rainfall over Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu and Kashmir for the next two days. According to IMD, heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected over Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Assam, Meghalaya, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim today.
Heavy rainfall is likely over Chhattisgarh, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Jharkhand, Kerala, Mahe, Konkan, Goa and Madhya Maharashtra for the next three days. Similar conditions are expected over Madhya Pradesh, Odisha and northeastern states over the period.
The Met Department has forecast thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds over Ladakh, Vidarbha, Karnataka, Telangana, Rayalaseema and Andaman and Nicobar Islands over the next three days. Parts of Delhi witnessed a brief spell of light to very light rainfall early this morning. The weather agency has forecast moderate to light rainfall during the next three days.

title: IMD predicts severe heatwave conditions over North, West India; heavy rainfall in NE region - News On AIR
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heat wave to severe heat wave conditions over West Madhya Pradesh and West Rajasthan for the next two days. East Rajasthan, Gujarat and Maharashtra are expected to reel under heat wave conditions tomorrow. The Weather agency also predicted heavy to very heavy rainfall over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya tomorrow. According to IMD, heavy rainfall is also likely over Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Kerala, Mahe, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura, Sub Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal during the next three days. Places over Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Chandigarh, Odisha, West Bengal and the Northeastern states are expected to experience thunderstorms and lightning tomorrow. According to the Met department, thundersquall conditions are expected during the next 2 days over Jharkhand, Coastal Karnataka, and North Interior Karnataka.
IMD further said that conditions are becoming favourable for the advance of southwest monsoon over parts of south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea and Andaman and Nicobar Islands around Saturday.
Meanwhile, the national capital experienced a light spell of rain showers accompanied by strong winds this evening.
